Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable Relocations table (appMoveOpsOps001, tblRelocations01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Tyler Okonwo" (or similar). Expected findings: record recTylerOkonwoPhillyDenver showing Canopy Health, Philadelphia to Denver, Apr 12-14, status In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, notes about temporary storage required (~450 cu ft, vacate Apr 18, lease starts May 1), storage quote pending, and high-value items ($3,500 bike, 65-inch TV).
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Tyler Okonwo", "Denver storage", or "Chloe Vance" (or similar) to find the storage sourcing instructions. Expected finding: email_chloe_javier_briefing_001 from Chloe Vance to Javier (cc Catalina Dubois), instructing him to source a Denver-area short-term storage facility for ~450 cu ft (~2 weeks, April 14–May 1), get a quote, and send it to Chloe and Catalina before the invoice goes out.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices or similar with queries related to "Canopy Health" or "INV-2026-0401" (or similar) to find the billing dependency. Expected findings: invoice INV-2026-0401 (QuickBooks ID 1009) totaling $9,210, covering Kovacs, Webb, and Okonwo relocations plus surcharges. The Okonwo line includes a $600 temporary storage fee already on the invoice but marked "PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL" the storage quote exists but awaits client sign-off before the invoice can be finalised.
OE 4: Search Airtable Relocations table (appMoveOpsOps001, tblRelocations01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Keiko Tanaka" (or similar). Expected findings: record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle showing Mosaic Robotics, Columbus to Seattle, Apr 15-23, status In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, vendor Swift Relocations, notes about a $35,000 Epilog Fusion Pro laser cutter (400 lbs, Class 3B CO2) requiring DOT hazmat placarding, $800 hazmat surcharge, $450 storage fee, and an 8-day storage gap.
OE 5: Search Linear for Tanaka or DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Tanaka", "DOT", or "compliance" (or similar). Expected findings: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (#2) under project_dot_compliance_audit showing hazmat placarding certificate status MISSING, Blessing Okafor obtained only a verbal confirmation from the Swift driver with no written cert. Also discover subtask issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (#5) in state_todo to get Greg Pallone at Swift to provide the retroactive placarding certificate.
OE 6: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Tanaka", "hazmat", "Swift", or "placarding" (or similar) to find hazmat communications. Expected findings: Chloe Vance's existing email to Greg Pallone at Swift (chloe_email_swift_placarding_cert) requesting the signed placarding certificate, Greg's reply confirming the placarding was completed and that the signed cert is being pulled from Swift's compliance archive with PDFs expected within 24 hours, and Chloe Vance's Slack messages in #operations (C006) documenting the DOT compliance audit status including the finding that Blessing Okafor handled the Tanaka hazmat coordination (not Javier) and obtained only a verbal confirmation from the Swift driver with no follow-up for the written certificate. Note: the detail about Blessing's verbal-only confirmation is also documented in the Linear ticket issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic discovered in OE 5.
OE 7: Search Airtable Relocations table (appMoveOpsOps001, tblRelocations01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Danielle Osei" (or similar). Expected findings: record rec_reloc_osei showing Vectral Systems, Phoenix to Denver, status Cancelled, with notes stating "Cancellation email sent to Heartland (Jake Loomis) on 2/13. No acknowledgment received from vendor."
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Osei", "cancellation", "Heartland", or "Jake Loomis" (or similar) to find the cancellation correspondence. Expected finding: email_cancel_osei_heartland the cancellation email Chloe Vance sent to Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) on February 13, requesting removal from Heartland's Q1 schedule and confirmation that no charges apply. No reply or acknowledgment from Jake Loomis exists in the email history.
OE 9: Search Linear for open Osei tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Osei" or "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: three tickets still active despite the cancelled relocation: issue_vectral_osei_apt (#310, apartment search, state_in_progress), issue_vectral_osei_flight (#311, flight booking, state_todo), and issue_vectral_osei_movers (#312, moving coordination, state_todo).
OE 10: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with queries for "Greg Pallone", "Jake Loomis", "Chloe Vance", and "Catalina Dubois" (or similar). Expected findings: Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, National Account Manager at Swift Relocations), Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com, Director Corporate Accounts at Heartland Movers), Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com), and Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com).
OE 11: Send a follow-up email (via send_email) to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com referencing Chloe's existing outreach about the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment, the Class 3B CO2 hazmat placarding requirement under 49 CFR 172.504, and requesting expedited delivery of the signed certificate. CC chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 12: Send an email (via send_email) to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com requesting written confirmation that the Danielle Osei relocation (Phoenix to Denver) has been cancelled on Heartland's end, referencing the original cancellation sent by Chloe Vance on February 13, and asking Jake to confirm the truck/crew slot has been released.
OE 13: Send an email (via send_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com and catalina.dubois@moveops.com reporting on Tyler Okonwo's storage situation, the 450 cu ft requirement for approximately April 18 through May 1, and the dependency on Canopy invoice INV-2026-0401 which already includes a $600 pending storage line item awaiting client approval.
OE 14: Update Keiko Tanaka's Airtable relocation record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) using airtable_update_records to add notes about the DOT compliance gap hazmat placarding certificate missing, remediation in progress, follow-up sent to Swift.
OE 15: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the Tanaka DOT compliance Linear ticket (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic or subtask issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert) documenting that only verbal confirmation was obtained, the written certificate is outstanding, and a follow-up email has been sent to Greg Pallone requesting expedited delivery.
OE 16: Update all three Danielle Osei Linear tickets using linear_update_issue issue_vectral_osei_apt (#310), issue_vectral_osei_flight (#311), and issue_vectral_osei_movers (#312) changing status to cancelled with a note that the move was cancelled by Vectral in February and vendor confirmation is being sought from Heartland Movers.
OE 17: Create a new Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) to track Tyler Okonwo's Denver storage sourcing, capturing the 450 cu ft requirement, April 18–May 1 window, high-value items requiring special handling, and the invoice dependency.
OE 18: Post a summary message in #operations Slack channel (C006) using conversations_add_message, flagging all three open threads, Tyler Okonwo's storage quote is still pending with the Canopy invoice blocked on client approval, Keiko Tanaka's hazmat placarding certificate is missing with DOT violation risk, and Danielle Osei's cancellation remains unconfirmed by Heartland with orphaned Linear tickets still open. Note that vendor outreach is in progress for the Tanaka and Osei situations.
OE 19: Respond to Javier summarizing findings across all three threads the Tyler Okonwo storage situation and its dependency on Canopy invoice finalization, the Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance gap including the missing placarding cert, Blessing's verbal-only confirmation, potential 49 CFR violation, and the cert incoming from Swift, and the Danielle Osei cancellation gap with no vendor acknowledgment from Heartland and orphaned tickets now closed. Confirm all actions taken including emails sent, records updated, tickets created/closed, and the ops channel notified.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email is sent to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations ([greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com)) requesting the hazmat placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter shipment from Columbus to Seattle.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows send_email was called with recipient greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com referencing the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment from Columbus to Seattle, requesting the hazmat placarding certificate.
✓Run #2The agent called send_email with 'to' field containing greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, with subject referencing 'Keiko Tanaka Shipment' and body referencing Columbus to Seattle hazmat placarding certificate.
✓Run #3The agent sent an email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com referencing the Keiko Tanaka relocation (Columbus to Seattle) and requesting the hazmat placarding certificate documents.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a send_email call with recipients: ['greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com'], subject 'Follow-Up: Signed Placarding Certificate PDFs — Keiko Tanaka Shipment (SR-2026-04-1538)', and the email body explicitly references the Keiko Tanaka relocation (Columbus to Seattle) and requests the hazmat placarding certificate.
✓Run #5The agent called send_email with recipients=['greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com'], subject referencing 'Keiko Tanaka Shipment' and the email body explicitly requests the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka Columbus-to-Seattle shipment.
✓Run #6The agent sent an email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com with subject 'Follow-Up: Keiko Tanaka Placarding Certificate — Confirming Receipt of Signed Documents'. The email body explicitly references the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics Columbus-to-Seattle relocation and requests the placarding certificate (scanned PDFs). CC'd chloe.vance@moveops.com and blessing.okafor@moveops.com.
2 The email to Greg Pallone references the Class 3B CO2 laser or Epilog Fusion Pro equipment requiring hazmat placarding.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The email to Greg Pallone references 'laser cutter shipment' but does not explicitly mention 'Class 3B', 'CO2 laser', or 'Epilog Fusion Pro'. Despite the agent having accessed the Airtable record and Linear ticket containing these details, the email body omits the specific equipment classification.
✓Run #2The email to Greg Pallone explicitly references 'Class 3B CO2 laser' and 'Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538', identifying the specific equipment requiring hazmat placarding.
✗Run #3The email to Greg Pallone references the 'laser cutter shipment' but does not explicitly mention 'Class 3B', 'CO2 laser', or 'Epilog Fusion Pro'. The agent had access to this detail from the Linear issue but did not include it in the email body.
✗Run #4The email to Greg Pallone refers to 'the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment' but never mentions 'Class 3B', 'CO2 laser', or 'Epilog Fusion Pro' in the email body. The criterion requires at least one of these identifiers to appear in the email. The agent had this information available from the Linear ticket (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) but did not include it in the email.
✗Run #5The email to Greg Pallone refers only to 'Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment' generically. It does not include the specific terms 'Class 3B', 'CO2 laser', or 'Epilog Fusion Pro' that would identify the particular hazmat classification. The Airtable record contained this detail but the agent did not include it in the email.
✗Run #6The email to Greg Pallone references 'the Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver' and 'DOT placarding certificate' but does not specifically mention 'Class 3B', 'CO2 laser', or 'Epilog Fusion Pro'. Despite having this information from the Airtable record and Linear ticket, the agent omitted specific equipment classification details from the email.
3 The email to Greg Pallone references DOT compliance requirements or the applicable hazmat regulation (49 CFR 172.504).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Greg Pallone explicitly references '49 CFR 172, Subpart F' (the hazmat placarding regulation) and mentions a 'DOT compliance audit', satisfying the requirement to reference DOT compliance or applicable hazmat regulations.
✓Run #2The email references 'DOT hazmat placarding certificate', '49 CFR 172, Subpart F', and 'PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187', grounding the request in regulatory requirements.
✓Run #3The email to Greg Pallone explicitly references '49 CFR 172, Subpart F' and requests a 'Signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate', grounding the request in regulatory requirements.
✓Run #4The email to Greg Pallone references 'DOT Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187', describes it as a 'federal compliance deadline', and explicitly cites 'Signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate (49 CFR 172, Subpart F)'. This constitutes regulatory language grounding the request in DOT compliance requirements.
✓Run #5The email to Greg Pallone explicitly requests '1. Signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate (49 CFR 172, Subpart F)' and references Swift PHMSA registration, meeting the requirement for DOT compliance regulatory language.
✓Run #6The email to Greg Pallone states 'We're working against the extended DOT response deadline' and the subject itself references the 'Placarding Certificate'. The email body references the 'DOT audit response', constituting a reference to DOT compliance requirements and hazmat placarding documentation.
4 The email to Greg Pallone specifies the need for a written or signed certificate, distinguishing from the verbal-only confirmation previously obtained.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✓✗ 17%

30 The agent must execute a search query in Linear that surfaces the DOT compliance audit project or Tanaka-specific compliance tickets, rather than only searching for the relocation by name.
query construction
 ✓✗✗✗✗✗ 17%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows linear_list_issues was called with query 'placarding' (a compliance-oriented term), which returned the DOT compliance audit tickets. The agent also searched with 'Keiko Tanaka' but crucially also used the compliance-specific 'placarding' query.
✗Run #2All Linear searches used name-based queries ('Keiko Tanaka', 'Danielle Osei', 'Tyler Okonkwo', 'Osei') rather than compliance-oriented terms like 'DOT', 'hazmat', 'placarding', or 'audit'. The DOT compliance ticket was found incidentally through the 'Keiko Tanaka' search, not a compliance-focused query.
✗Run #3The agent's Linear searches used name-based queries ('Keiko Tanaka', 'Danielle Osei', 'Osei', etc.) rather than compliance-oriented terms like 'DOT', 'hazmat', 'placarding', or 'compliance'. The agent directly accessed issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic by ID without first discovering it through a compliance-oriented search query.
✗Run #4All Linear searches used name-based queries only: 'Osei', 'Tanaka', and 'Okonkwo'. No search used compliance-oriented terms such as 'DOT', 'compliance', 'hazmat', 'placarding', or 'audit'. The agent found the DOT ticket because it happened to be returned by the 'Tanaka' search, not because of a compliance-oriented query.
✗Run #5The agent searched Linear using query 'Tanaka' (name-only), not compliance-oriented terms like 'DOT', 'compliance', 'hazmat', 'placarding', or 'audit'. Per the criterion, full credit requires a compliance-oriented query. The agent did find the DOT ticket through the name search, but this only partially meets the criterion.
✗Run #6The agent's Linear searches used name-based queries only: 'Keiko Tanaka', 'Danielle Osei', and 'Tyler Okonkwo'. No compliance-oriented terms such as 'DOT', 'compliance', 'hazmat', 'placarding', or 'audit' were used in any Linear query. The criterion requires full credit only for compliance-oriented queries, with partial credit if the ticket was found by name. Since we must score binary, and full credit requires a compliance-oriented query, this fails.
31 The model must use quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice, or an equivalent QuickBooks read tool to look up the Canopy Health invoice
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No QuickBooks tool calls appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent discovered invoice INV-2026-0401 from Hana Kim's Slack message in the #operations channel, not through any QuickBooks search or read tool.
✗Run #2The agent used quickbooks_search_bills (not quickbooks_search_invoices) and only retrieved vendor bills. No call to quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice targeting Canopy Health or INV-2026-0401 (QuickBooks ID 1009) appears in the trajectory.
✗Run #3The agent attempted quickbooks_search_bills (which errored) but never called quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice. The Canopy Health invoice INV-2026-0401 was never discovered through QuickBooks.
✗Run #4The agent attempted quickbooks_search_bills which returned an error ('list object has no attribute get'). The agent never called quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_read_invoice, or any equivalent QuickBooks invoice read tool. As a result, invoice INV-2026-0401 and the pending $600 storage line item were never discovered.
✗Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (vendor bills/payables) and quickbooks_search_vendors, but never called quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice for customer invoices. The Canopy Health invoice INV-2026-0401 (QB ID 1009) was never looked up, which is why the agent could not reference the specific invoice number.
✗Run #6The agent used quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_vendors but not quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice. The Canopy Health invoice INV-2026-0401 is an accounts receivable invoice (not a bill), and the agent's QuickBooks searches only covered payables (bills and vendors). The Canopy invoice with its pending $600 storage line item was never found.
